evidence.description: Bahramani was established as a new Iranian contact in August 1986, meeting frequently with Oliver North in West Germany to discuss arms sales, hostage releases, and cooperation against Iraq.

evidence.source_title: Understanding the Iran-Contra Affairs — Brown University
evidence.description: Described as 'a relative of the Speaker of the Iranian Parliament,' Bahramani served as the 'second channel' after American frustration with Ghorbanifar.

editorial_notes: Known as 'the Relative' or 'second channel' in U.S. documents. Met North in West Germany. Given midnight White House tour. Approx. 20 years old.
